title: A Tasmanian Devil Named Mary Escaped. 6 Days Later, She's Still Out There.
script: She's loose! Mary, a 2-year-old Tasmanian devil, broke out of her enclosure at Paradise Country wildlife park on Queensland's Gold Coast on June 3. Zookeepers say it's a mystery — they think one abnormally large leap sent her over the fence after midnight. Tracker dogs, thermal drones, and wildlife experts searched for days. On June 8, a resident's home security camera caught Mary alive and roaming nearby bush. Tasmanian devils are critically endangered on Australia's mainland — fewer than 30 are believed to live in Queensland. Mary is described as shy, but the park warns she can be reactive if cornered or if anyone tries to catch her. Has Australia's most famous fugitive marsupial finally met her match in the Gold Coast suburbs?
